transcript: Yes, this is Humphrey. I’m calling from—
—Downtown Digital Destruction. How are ya today, sir?
We’ve been up ’n down the block. We got several people on our route. And we’d love to work with you guys! [exhales] Shredding, and—
—Well, I’m not—I—I think we do have a shredder that’s doing it, but, uh, if you can do it—

—Well, you know, earlier in the week, we were there. You were out at the time. But we picked up some documents from your office and destroyed those. So we’d love to come and show you what we’ve done. They weren’t important or anything.
Well, y—y—you’re talkin’ to the wrong guy, even though it’s my firm. You’re gonna talk to, uh—uh, either, ah, Michael, who’s my administrator or you call Sharon, who’s my bookkeeper—
—Well Sharon told me to talk to you, so [exhales]
Well, sir, I’m telling you that I don’t know the answer. I don’t get competitive bids; I don’t get into that.
We already des—[reverb & delay] We already destroyed a lot of your stuff and we’re—wanna just present it to ya. We also have reconstructive services—
We can actually reconstruct destroyed documents. That’s running you just a total of $90 per hour. It’s forensic level stuff we’re doin’ here [exhales] [piano note]
Sir, I’m not questioning your capabilities—
—I’m telling you that I am not able to make a decision without knowing what we’re paying now—
—and what you’re offering to pay and things of that sort. So—
—Well we’ll destroy, ah, 50 pounds o’ stuff no charge; it’s a coupon that we’ve got. And my name’s Humphrey; I’m the owner of the company. Okay? [reverb & delay]—
—I’ve told you three times already. You can keep tellin’ me how good you are—
—but it’s a waste of your time and mine. If you wanna call Sharon, my office next week—when—when did you—when were you in the office?—

—I’ve already spoken to her. We took a buncha documents from your office, destroyed ‘em, and we’re just gonna come show ya how good a job we did. It’s real quick; you’re gonna love what ya see! Okay? [reverb & delay] Real good stuff. [piano note] And we’ve got a coupon, like I said. So you’re gonna save a lot of money. You can appreciate that, can’t ye?—
—Okay. No, I can’t, because I can’t seem to get through to you, sir—
—That I am not in a position to make that decision—certainly not now on a Saturday afternoon. Now, you can do one of two things—
What’s that we can do? What’re my choices here, hm? [sfx]
Your choices are: hang up, number one, and never call me again—
—or make an appointment—yeah—or make an appointment; make an appointment to see Sharon and show her what you’ve done. And—
—We’ve destroyed everything in sight, is what we’ve done, and it’s dynamite!—
—Sir, I’m done; I’m done talkin’ to you. Bye-bye!—
—I’m—I’m gonna be there in five minutes, okay?
Yes, Bob, this is Helmut calling.
Oh, “Hel—“! I just got a harassing call from somebody and I don’t know—it had to do with destroying documents and his name was very similar to yours. Anyway, go ahead. I’m sorry.
Okay! So we’re just gonna come by, uh, to your residence in the next 15, 20 minutes and give ya a brief presentation, okay? On our destructive and our reconstructive services that we offer—
—Oh, no, you are the same guy I just talked to, right?—
—[reverb & delay] Oh, oh! Okay, yes! Mhm, yeah! So whatever we can do for ya—
—Alright, do you want me to call the—do—do—do you want me to call the police? You call me one more time—
—Well, you want me to get rough, guy? I tried to offer ya a discount—
Sir, you are—you are—I am not interested in doing business with you! Do not call me again!
I’m gonna shove you around if I have to; how does that sound, here?—
—Well, you—you shove me around, okay? You shove me around! Y—y—you sound like a crazy man right now, alright? So I don’t wanna have anything to do with you, you understand?
What do you want from me, here? I’m a businessman—
—I want you to never call me again; that’s what I want—
—I don’t care! You sound like—you sound like you belong—
[emergency siren] I’ll perform Tae Kwon Do on ya, on your lips! [exhales] [reverb & delay]—
—Yeah. Fine. You sound like a nutcase—
I’m gonna destroy everything I see! [siren]
Hi, this is Humphrey; I work at Downtown Digital Destruction; we do all the data up ’n down the block, here; all ’n throughout the county! I’m going to see you—
—Yeah, I got your call before, yeah! I—I got your call, yeah! Come on over!—
—Don’t let anybody bother ya—the police are outside—don’t let that bother you!—
—Don’t let the FBI bother you, either, okay? ‘Cause they’re nice people!—
—Don’t let the door hit ya in the backside.
Yeah. I—I understand, Humphrey. You called me once before and I think we had the same discussion.
I’m gonna meet you at 9 AM—
—and we’re gonna demonstrate what we do when we destroy your data!—
I appreciate you more than you think—
We also do restorations; so we can put back together—
—any documents that you’ve mistakenly destroyed or shredded! [train clattering]
Didn’t you tell me last time that you’d already been to my office destroying some data, Humphrey?—
Hey, how ‘bout I come up there and shove you around a little bit?
Well why don’t you—why don’t you—why don’t you do that, Humphrey? And—and, ah, I’ll be there for you, waiting.
[speaking to Bob] Stop. There’s gonna be a car comin’ down this driveway.
—It’s not a problem, Humphrey. You just come on over and beat the hell outta me and whatever else you wanna do—
—[siren] It’s gonna be a big problem for your lips once I connect with you!—
—Alright, I understand that, Humphrey. But—but, you know, guys like you need to be put away in a special place. Special place!—
—I’m a businessman; I’m a patriot and I’m a businessman—
—No, you’re not! You’re a crook-scam-fraud guy! That’s what you are, Humphrey! You’re a real weird guy. You must have some psychiatric problems as well, Humphrey—
—and I’m gonna make a presentation; you’re gonna love what ya see—end of story!—
—Yeah! I—I told ya—I told ya, Humphrey, to come on over! Wha—what more do I gotta say?
Just make a small deposit and then we’ll put ya on retainer—
—Oh yeah! Listen, tell me how much you want; you want five thousand, ten thousand? How much you want, Humphrey?—
—No. Six months is $970 for six months of service!—
—24 hours a day we’ll come out—seven days a week! We’re the best!—
—I—Humphrey, that’s a felony; what you’re doing is a felony.
And I think—not only is it a felony, Humphrey, but I think that you crossed interstate lines and I think you’ll end up in a federal prison; how’s that sound?
They’re comin’ to get you right now.
Yeah, that’s—in fact, I’m gonna call somebody right now and trace your number and, uh, uh, report you to the Feds—
—‘cause this is the second time you bothered me, Humphrey, okay?—
—You’re bananas! You hear me? Fella, you’re bananas!—
—and—and you’re a fruitcake; how’s that sound?
I’m bananas and you’re a fruitcake!; how’s that sound, Humphrey?—
—I’m a patriot and I’m a businessman and I’m a businessman [unintelligible]—
—No, you’re not! You’re a scumbag is what you are, Humphre—Humphrey, and you need to be put away someplace where there’s a big lock and key; that’s what they need to do with you.
Who’s talkin’ in the background here?—
—So you know what, Humphrey? You bothered me enough for me to take some action, okay?
You bothered me enough for me to stop guys like you from buggin’ people. Just beware, Humphrey. Okay? ‘Cause you’re a scumbag and a—a fraud and a sick guy, okay? Other than that, you’re a fruitcake—
—So pick the choice: are you a fruitcake, a scumbag, a fraud, a scam, or a conman; which one do you think fits you the best?
I’m gonna kick ya in the kneecap; that’s what I’m gonna do tomorrow—
—Yeah, you kick me—you kick me in the kneecap—fr—yeah—come on right over and kick me in the kneecap, okay?
—I’m gonna take you down to the ground, is what I’m gonna do; take ya down!—

—Yeah! You—you do—you do that and just ca—you just threatened me for another felony! So you’ve got a couple felonies lined up, okay? You threatened me and you’ve also scammed me; so that oughta put you in state prison—Jackson or someplace like that—for about 15 years! Okay, so, you just keep threatening me, scumbag and I will pursue you ’til you are put away.
Just make the deposit and we’ll put ya on retainer—end of story, alright?—
—Oh, no, no! I’ll make a deposit o—I’ll make a deposit on your neck, okay? And make sure that you’re put away someplace where you belong, Humphrey—
—I’m gonna take a block o’ wood to your chinbone. See what ya say then, guy—
Hi—hey—hey, that sounds good. Yeah. Fuck off! Yeah, right! Listen: you just take your data and stick it up your rear; how does that sound? Does that sound okay to you? Stick your data up your rear?—
—And—and maybe you oughta destroy the data up your rear!
‘Cause I—I—I enjoy talkin’ to you, Humphrey. ‘Cause you’re—you’re a sick man, besides being a crook and a scumbag. Okay?
—and I’m trying to do you a favor!—
You’re a scumbag! You’re a scumbag and a—and a fraud and a—and a fruitcake and, ah, one of the worst people that I’ve had a chance to talk to.

Yeah, I’m—I’m not s—yeah, well f—Humphrey, I’m gonna—I’m gonna trace you down, now. I’m gonna file a complaint with the FBI. ‘Cause you’re using interstate commerce and I’m gonna have ‘em trace you down. Okay, Humphrey? We’ll see who ends up battering who and who ends up in jail! You will be out of commission! Okay?
Oh, I’ll prevail! No doubt in my mind. No doubt in my mind!—

—Oh yeah, you’ll prevail. You sound like you’re off the wall anyway! Yeah, I’m gonna precipitate your demise, Humphrey. You just crossed the line. This is the second time you bugged me and you have crossed the line! So you wanna find out whether I’m serious or not? You’ll find out very soon. ‘Cause I’m not taking this crap from some scumbag like you anymore! Anymore!

—I—I—I’m not gonna just check ya out, I’m gonna file a federal complaint against you, that’s what I’m gonna do! I’m gonna call the FBI and I’m gonna tell ‘em you threatened me on the phone—both physically and otherwise—and you have a scam operating and that you are crossing state lines and you violated various federal statutes. Okay, that’s what’ I’m gonna tell ‘em.
Yeah, whenever they find you and put you away, I’m going to ask them to tell me where you’re locked up. And you know what, Humphrey? I’m gonna come and visit you!—
—In jail! That’s what I’m gonna do. So then you can sell me some data from the jailhouse and I’ll, ah, be a—
—excellent witness for the prosecution against a scumbag like you. So you just wait for the door to knock, okay? And when the door knocks, you’re gonna know who called. Okay, Humphrey?
No, why not? You’re a scumbag! You’re a fraud and a crook and a felon; you’re a felon! You’ve already committed two felonies, okay?—
—That’ll be it for you, Humphrey—whatever your damn name is—
—We’ll find out what your name is—
—We’ll find out what your name is, Humph—
—I’ve been in business for nine years!
No, you’re outta business! This was the end. You will be out of business very shortly.

Okay. That’ll be a problem for your family if you have one. But I doubt you have a family because I think they would have committed you by now. So you probably don’t have a family. But that’s okay, Humphrey. Not everybody gets to have a family, and not everybody gets to stay out of jail. So—
Humphrey, this’ll be the worst call you’ve ever made, okay? You picked the wrong guy to screw around with!—
Oh yeah! Y—yeah, you have, Humphrey. Or what the hell your name is—
—I’m gonna call ya, “1-2-7-7-1”; that’s your new number in jail. 1-2-7-7-1. Write that down, ‘cause that’s gonna be your new number: 1-2-7-7-1!
Guy, I’m no nonsense! Period, end ‘o sentence!
